fr:mainmatter.fr:p: The coproduct of lift-algebras is computed as a reflexive coequalizer, whose existence we have already established. Given an index set I and a diagram {I}\xrightarrow {{X_{\bullet }}}{\mathsf {dcpo}^{\mathbb {L}}}, the colimit of the following reflexive pair is the coproduct of {I}\xrightarrow {{X_{\bullet }}}{\mathsf {dcpo}^{\mathbb {L}}} as shown by Linton:
The Eilenberg–Moore category \mathsf {dcpo}^{\mathbb {L}} for the lifting monad \mathbb {L}={\mathopen {}\left (\mathsf {L},\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} on dcpos has all small coproducts.
This is the first part of Corollary 2 of Linton's Coequalizers in categories of algebras.

The coproduct of lift-algebras is computed as a reflexive coequalizer, whose existence we have already established. Given an index set I and a diagram {I}\xrightarrow {{X_{\bullet }}}{\mathsf {dcpo}^{\mathbb {L}}}, the colimit of the following reflexive pair is the coproduct of {I}\xrightarrow {{X_{\bullet }}}{\mathsf {dcpo}^{\mathbb {L}}} as shown by Linton:

Let \mathbb {L}={\mathopen {}\left (\mathsf {L},\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be the lifting monad on dcpos; the Eilenberg–Moore category \mathsf {dcpo}^{\mathbb {L}} is closed under connected colimits, and these colimits are created by the forgetful functor {\mathsf {dcpo}^{\mathbb {L}}}\xrightarrow {{\mathsf {U}^{\mathbb {L}}}}{\mathsf {dcpo}}.
This follows from the following facts:

fr:ol.fr:li: The category of dcpos is (even constructively) known to be cocomplete.
The forgetful functor {\mathsf {dcpo}^{\mathbb {L}}}\xrightarrow {{\mathsf {U}^{\mathbb {L}}}}{\mathsf {dcpo}} creates whatever colimits \mathsf {L} preserves.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Coequalizers in categories of algebras
fr:frontmatter.fr:meta: Seminar on Triples and Categorical Homology Theory

@inproceedings{linton-1969-coequalizers, address = {Berlin, Heidelberg}, author = {Linton, F. E. J.}, booktitle = {Seminar on Triples and Categorical Homology Theory}, editor = {Eckmann, B.}, isbn = {978-3-540-36091-9}, pages = {75--90}, publisher = {Springer Berlin Heidelberg}, title = {Coequalizers in categories of algebras}, year = {1969} }
